description:
Recto: Fragment from the bottom of a communal letter, with wide line spacing. Dated: Adar 1463 Seleucid = February/March 1152 CE. Potentially from the office of the Nagid (Shemuel b. Ḥananya). Ends with the phrase "what we have written is sufficient" (ופי מא סטרנאה כפאיה), cf. T-S 10J20.21 (PGPID 2950). Issuing an instruction or warning that no Jew should [...] and that everyone should cease harming other people. The details are unknown. (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)

Verso: Fragment from the top of a document in Arabic script, probably a letter. Part of the basmala is preserved and the phrase "ʿan tafaḍḍul."
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-12. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
